questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: Turn curiosity into capability — learn Notion, build smarter workflows, and validate your skills with badges & certifications.
options.explanation: This sentence appears verbatim in the article as the mission statement for Notion Academy.

options.text: Provide a marketplace for buying and selling third‑party Notion templates.
options.explanation: The marketplace is mentioned as a lesson topic, not the overall purpose of the Academy.

options.text: Offer live technical support for troubleshooting Notion errors.
options.explanation: Live support is offered via webinars and the Help Center, but not the Academy’s core purpose.

options.text: Serve as a repository for all Notion documentation and API references.
options.explanation: Documentation lives in the Help Center; the Academy focuses on learning and certification.

questions.question: Which learning path is described as “New to Notion? Gain the basic skills to create, collaborate, and organize your work efficiently.”?

options.explanation: The article lists the Essentials Path with exactly that description.
Workflows Path focuses on creating connected systems, not on introductory basics.
Advanced Path is for deeper automation and workspace architecture, not for beginners.
There is a lesson about templates, but not a dedicated path with that description.

questions.question: Which lesson in the list is categorized as an “Advanced” difficulty?

options.explanation: The article tags the “Workspace design” lesson as Advanced.
Building basics is marked as Beginner.
Custom Agents is listed as Beginner.
Sharing & collaboration is also a Beginner lesson.

questions.question: How many individual lessons are included in the Advanced Path?

options.explanation: The Advanced Path contains more than three lessons.
Four lessons belong to the Workflows Path, not the Advanced Path.
The article states the Advanced Path consists of 5 lessons.
Six lessons are not mentioned for any path.

questions.question: Which lesson specifically teaches how to create, list, market, and sell templates in Notion’s Marketplace?

options.explanation: The lesson title and description match this exact focus.
Project management covers setting up projects and task databases, not templates.
That lesson is about teamwork, not marketplace activities.
Custom Agents deals with automating repeat work, unrelated to template sales.

questions.question: What is the listed duration for the “Custom Agents” lesson?

options.explanation: 15 minutes is the duration for some other lessons, such as Sharing & collaboration.
20 minutes applies to Building basics and Connecting knowledge in Notion.
The article lists Custom Agents as a 30‑minute, Beginner lesson.
40 minutes is the duration for the Templates & marketplace lesson.

questions.question: According to the article, what can you earn by completing a Notion Academy path?

options.explanation: The article explicitly states you can “earn badges with Notion” by taking paths.
Free templates are not mentioned as a reward for completing paths.
Subscriptions are unrelated to Academy completion.
Coaching sessions are not listed as a benefit of completing a path.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a benefit of becoming a Notion Certified Admin?

options.explanation: Running Notion at scale is explicitly mentioned as a benefit.
Security best practices are listed among the certification benefits.

options.text: Get recognized as a certified Notion Admin
options.explanation: Recognition as a certified admin is part of the certification description.
The article never mentions free premium templates as a certification benefit.
